name: Claude Toolkit Provider-Alias Verification Anti-Bluff Bank

metadata.description: Anti-bluff invariants of the fixed claude_toolkit provider-alias verification pipeline. The pipeline previously reported aliases as verified from a bare GET /v1/models 200, so providers with dead completions (e.g. huggingface with depleted credits) passed verification while being unusable at runtime. Each test case encodes one honesty gate of the fixed pipeline and is executed via challenges/scripts/provider_aliases_challenge.sh, which inspects the toolkit checkout READ-ONLY and FAILs with positive evidence on drift.

test_cases.name: No verified-on-models-GET-only
test_cases.description: scripts/providers-verify.sh must not map a bare GET /v1/models HTTP 200 to status 'verified'. The models list endpoint answers even when every completion fails (billing, decommissioned model), so a GET 200 proves nothing about runtime usability. Verification must POST to a chat completion endpoint instead.

outputs.name: probe_http_method
outputs.description: HTTP method the verification probe uses (must be POST)

outputs.name: probe_endpoint
outputs.description: Endpoint path the probe targets (must be a chat completion endpoint)

outputs.name: bare_get_verified_paths
outputs.description: Code paths mapping a bare GET /v1/models response to 'verified' (must be empty)

assertions.message: verification probe must POST to a chat endpoint; a bare GET /v1/models 200 is the huggingface depleted-credits false-positive class
probe must hit a real chat completion endpoint, not the models list
no code path may map a bare GET /v1/models response to status verified

test_cases.name: Sentinel VERIFY_OK asserted
test_cases.description: scripts/model_verify.py probes with an exact-token demand ('Reply with exactly: VERIFY_OK') and must ASSERT the sentinel against the response content before a model can score as verified. Defining EXPECTED_CONTENT without a membership assertion lets any 200 — error string, proxy fallback, canned text — pass as verified.

outputs.name: sentinel_literal
outputs.description: The exact token the probe demands (VERIFY_OK)

outputs.name: sentinel_assertion_sites
outputs.description: Locations where the sentinel is asserted against response content

outputs.name: unasserted_verified_paths
outputs.description: Paths to 'verified' that skip the sentinel assertion (must be empty)

assertions.message: the probe must demand the exact VERIFY_OK token
the sentinel must be asserted against response content at least once (membership test, not mere definition)
no path to 'verified' may skip the sentinel assertion

test_cases.name: Tool calling required for verified
test_cases.description: Claude Code is entirely tool-driven, so tool calling is a hard verification gate, not a score component: scripts/model_verify.py must decide 'verified' in conjunction with the tool_call capability probe. A chat-only model that cannot call tools is useless to the aliases and must not verify.

outputs.name: tool_gate_evidence
outputs.description: Source evidence that verified decisions reference tool_call

outputs.name: verified_without_tool_call_paths
outputs.description: Paths to 'verified' that ignore the tool_call outcome (must be empty)

assertions.message: verified decisions must reference the tool_call capability
no path to 'verified' may bypass the tool-calling gate

test_cases.name: Billing / 402 is definitive failure, not skip
test_cases.description: Depleted credits (HTTP 402 or billing error bodies — the huggingface case) must be a DEFINITIVE failure with a recorded failing_layer, never an inconclusive 'unverified' skip and never a verified pass. model_verify.py flags billing signatures in ERROR_PATTERNS and treats HTTP 200 error bodies as bluffs; claude-providers.sh persists failing_layer for every non-verified alias.

outputs.name: billing_failure_signature
outputs.description: The billing error signature the verifier matches (must mention billing)

outputs.name: billing_verdict
outputs.description: Verdict recorded for billing-failing aliases (must be a definitive failure)

outputs.name: billing_skip_paths
outputs.description: Paths where a billing failure degrades to skip/unverified-without-failing_layer (must be empty)

assertions.message: the verifier must match billing/credits error signatures explicitly
billing failure must be a definitive failure verdict with failing_layer recorded
billing failure may never degrade to a silent skip

test_cases.name: Stale verified status detected
test_cases.description: The persisted status cache (~/.local/share/claude-multi-account/providers/status.json) must not carry 'verified' aliases whose checked_at is older than the staleness budget (7 days) or whose failing_layer is non-empty. Provider credit and model availability drift over time; a stale 'verified' is yesterday's truth presented as today's.

outputs.name: stale_verified_aliases
outputs.description: Verified aliases whose checked_at exceeds the staleness budget (must be empty)

outputs.name: verified_records_checked
outputs.description: All verified records inspected (must be non-empty for the gate to be meaningful)

outputs.name: checked_at_timestamps
outputs.description: checked_at values of verified records (all must be present and parseable)

assertions.message: no alias may stay 'verified' past the staleness budget
at least one verified record must be inspected — an empty set would make the gate vacuous
every verified record must carry a parseable checked_at timestamp

test_cases.name: Provider-aliases Challenge end-to-end honesty
test_cases.description: challenges/scripts/provider_aliases_challenge.sh runs against the toolkit checkout and returns an honest verdict: PASS only on positive evidence (unique per-run token, grep line numbers, jq tallies), SKIP-OK only when the checkout is genuinely absent, FAIL with named evidence otherwise. This case guards the guard.

outputs.name: challenge_verdict
outputs.description: Final verdict of the challenge run (PASS or honest SKIP-OK)

outputs.name: evidence_token
outputs.description: Unique per-run evidence token embedded in the verdict log

outputs.name: evidence_log
outputs.description: Full challenge output including evidence lines

assertions.message: challenge verdict must be PASS or an honest SKIP-OK — a FAIL here means the toolkit pipeline regressed
every run must mint a unique evidence token (defeats stale-cache false-pass)
the evidence log must carry the per-run token
evidence must be real grep/jq output, never placeholder text

test_cases.name: Kimi OAuth token is fresh at launch (live cred file + expiry + CLI refresh)
test_cases.description: The Kimi Code OAuth token lives ~15 minutes, so a sync-time snapshot 401s by the next launch. The launch path (lib.sh cma_run_provider) must read the LIVE credentials file (~/.kimi-code/credentials/kimi-code.json) when unexpired, refresh via `kimi -p hi` when expired, and only then fall back to the token-file snapshot. Verified by test_kimi.sh section 3 (three launch cases) and challenge check 5c.

outputs.name: freshness_verdict
outputs.description: pass when the launch path resolves the token from the live credentials file with expiry + CLI refresh

assertions.message: launch path must consult the live OAuth credentials file
expiry must gate the live-file read
CLI refresh path must exist before snapshot fallback
token source must be real (cred file / CLI refresh / snapshot), never assumed

test_cases.name: One provider alias per OAuth subscription-served Kimi model
test_cases.description: The Kimi Code subscription serves multiple models (k3 / Kimi 3, k2p7 / Kimi 2.7, kimi-for-coding-highspeed, kimi-for-coding default). The detector must discover them via GET {base}/models with the OAuth token and emit one provider record per model (never a single hardcoded alias), with OAuth records taking precedence over API-key records for kimi-for-coding. Verified by test_kimi.sh sections 1-2 and challenge checks 5a/5b.

outputs.name: records
outputs.description: detect_kimicode_record output — one record per served model

assertions.message: detector must query the /models discovery endpoint
merge must dedupe with first-wins semantics
at least the account-default record must be emitted when OAuth is present
records must come from live discovery or catalog fallback, never hardcoded

test_cases.name: kimi_proxy normalizes tool schemas to the moonshot #/$defs/ flavor
test_cases.description: Model k3 rejects any tool whose parameters carry a $ref not starting with #/$defs/ (400 'not a valid moonshot flavored json schema') — proven live: direct request 400s, the same request through kimi_proxy returns 200. The proxy must hoist $defs+definitions and rewrite foreign $refs by last segment. All kimi-* aliases route through it via the <family>_proxy.py discovery rule. Verified by test_kimi.sh section 4 and challenge check 5d.

outputs.name: normalized_schema
outputs.description: tool parameters with all $refs in #/$defs/ form

assertions.message: proxy must emit #/$defs/ refs
definitions blocks must be hoisted into $defs
normalization must transform real schemas, not pass-through
every tool parameters schema must be moonshot-valid after the proxy

test_cases.name: CLAUDE_CODE_MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS exported for both transports
test_cases.description: Root cause of 'Claude's response exceeded the 128000 output token maximum': the wrapper exported the output cap only on the native path, so router providers ran with Claude Code's generic default. The _cma_out_guard export must precede the router transport branch in lib.sh, the migration marker chain must include it (stale wrappers regenerate), and the cap must equal the provider model's real limit.output. Verified by test_output_tokens.sh (8 assertions) and challenge check 6.

outputs.name: cap_exported
outputs.description: CLAUDE_CODE_MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS value seen by both native and router launches

assertions.message: both-transports output-cap marker must exist in lib.sh
the documented output-cap env var must be exported
the cap must come from the provider's real limit.output, not a default

configuration.root_cause: router path never exported CLAUDE_CODE_MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS (128000 default error)

test_cases.name: Router launch is alias-proof and ccr-identity-guarded
test_cases.description: Two live router-path failures: bare `mv` in the ccr config upsert prompted under interactive `mv -i` shell aliases (launch hung/died), and a foreign `ccr` (CCS-style profile manager) made `ccr code` fail as 'Profile code was not found or is disabled'. lib.sh must use `command mv -f` at every mv site and refuse non-@musistudio ccr binaries before launch. Verified by test_output_tokens.sh section 3 and challenge check 6b.

outputs.name: hardening_verdict
outputs.description: pass when both hardenings are present in lib.sh

assertions.message: mv sites must be alias-proof
ccr identity guard must exist
the refusal must name the real router

test_cases.name: Sessions and background agents are visible from every alias
test_cases.description: Two root causes fixed in v1.17.0: (1) Claude Code's background-agent registry (daemon/roster.json) was per-config-dir, so agents were invisible to other aliases — daemon/ and jobs/ are now shared with a roster union merge (never last-wins) plus a provider-dir migration; (2) session resolution applied only to native-branch bare launches, so router aliases and `alias -p …` always started fresh — _cma_session_flags now covers both transports and injects --resume for conversation args via existing-id (never the never-created fallback). Live-proven: xiaomi -> kimi-k3 (router) -> deepseek all resumed the SAME session with shared memory.

outputs.name: continuity_verdict
outputs.description: pass when daemon is shared, flags unified, injection uses existing-id

assertions.message: CMA_SHARED_ITEMS must share daemon + jobs
roster union merge must exist
unified session flags marker must exist
args injection must use existing-id
all continuity checks must hold

configuration.live_proof: xiaomi -> kimi-k3 -> deepseek resumed identical session (2026-07-18)
